title: The exception handling in `_update_config` is wrong.
summary: In _update_config, after writing the config the code calls self.restart() and wraps it in `except GrafanaAgentReloadError` and `except APIError`. But for this machine (snap) charm restart() raises GrafanaAgentServiceError, not GrafanaAgentReloadError; APIError is a Pebble exception left over from the k8s charm and can never be raised here. So a real restart failure is not caught (and even if it were, BlockedStatus is wrong since the user cannot fix it - it should surface as an error).
why_good_first_issue: Small, precisely-described bug confined to one method; the issue body enumerates exactly the three things to change; fixable without architectural knowledge; very low existing test coverage of this path (only tests/unit/test_cert_transfer.py touches restart) so adding a focused unit test is a natural, self-contained deliverable.
repro_steps: On VM cos: git clone --depth 80 into ~/work; `sed -n '642,654p' src/grafana_agent.py` to read the try/except; `grep -n 'def restart' src/grafana_agent.py src/charm.py` and read the concrete restart() to see which exception it raises; `tox -l` confirms a `unit` env exists for adding a regression test.
repro_result: Confirmed on HEAD ad6f720. src/grafana_agent.py:644 calls `self.restart()`; src/grafana_agent.py:647 `except GrafanaAgentReloadError as e:` -> BlockedStatus; src/grafana_agent.py:650 `except APIError as e:` -> WaitingStatus. The base restart() (src/grafana_agent.py:405) is abstract; the concrete override raises GrafanaAgentServiceError (src/charm.py:431 and :438 'Failed to restart grafana-agent'). Therefore the caught exception types do not match what restart() actually raises -> a restart failure propagates uncaught. APIError is imported from ops.pebble (src/grafana_agent.py:40), a Pebble-only error, confirming it is dead handling in this snap charm. Code search shows no unit test exercises this except path.
fix_hint: In _update_config replace `except GrafanaAgentReloadError` with `except GrafanaAgentServiceError`, remove the `except APIError` clause (and drop the now-unused ops.pebble.APIError import if nothing else uses it), and surface the failure as an error/raise rather than BlockedStatus since it is not user-actionable. Add a unit test that makes restart() raise GrafanaAgentServiceError and asserts the resulting status/behaviour.
notes: Existing OPEN Type: Bug issue, still reproducible. The buggy block moved from src/charm.py (as quoted in the issue) to src/grafana_agent.py during refactor but is byte-for-byte the same logic, so the issue is current. Note the leftover FIXME on line 645 referencing #19.

title: Docs for refresh_events is incorrect
summary: The cos_agent.py library docstrings show `refresh_events=["update-status", "upgrade-charm"]` (string event names) as the usage example, and COSAgentRequirer type-hints refresh_events as Optional[List[str]]. But the code observes each entry via framework.observe, which requires BoundEvent objects (e.g. self.on.config_changed), so the documented string form raises `RuntimeError: Framework.observe requires a BoundEvent as second parameter`. The docs (prose, example, and type hint) should show event classes, and should note the default is on.config_changed.
why_good_first_issue: Pure documentation/type-hint fix in one library file; no behavioural change; the issue and a follow-up comment spell out exactly what to correct (examples, type hints, and document the config_changed default); ideal for a newcomer learning the ops event model.
repro_steps: On VM cos: git clone --depth 80 into ~/work; `grep -n 'refresh_events' lib/charms/grafana_agent/v0/cos_agent.py`; `sed -n '105,120p'` and `sed -n '958,968p'` to read the example and the type hint; `sed -n '673,682p'` to see the observe loop that requires BoundEvents.
repro_result: Confirmed on HEAD ad6f720. Docstring examples at cos_agent.py:114 and cos_agent.py:209 both show `refresh_events=["update-status", "upgrade-charm"]` (strings). COSAgentRequirer.__init__ type hint at cos_agent.py:964 is `refresh_events: Optional[List[str]] = None`. The init then does (cos_agent.py:680) a loop `for event in self._refresh_events: ... framework.observe(event, ...)`, and self._refresh_events defaults to `[self._charm.on.config_changed]` (cos_agent.py:673) - i.e. BoundEvents, not strings. So the documented string usage would crash exactly as the issue's RuntimeError shows. Observed: docs say strings; expected: event classes (and document the config_changed default).
fix_hint: Update both docstring examples to use event classes, e.g. `refresh_events=[self.on.update_status, self.on.config_changed]`; change the COSAgentRequirer type hint on cos_agent.py:964 from Optional[List[str]] to match the BoundEvent usage (consistent with the Optional[List] used elsewhere at line 39/631); add a sentence noting refresh_events defaults to on.config_changed. Bump LIBPATCH per charm-library convention.
notes: Existing OPEN issue (no Type label upstream but clearly a documentation bug). A maintainer-adjacent comment additionally requests documenting that refresh_events defaults to on.config_changed - captured in the fix hint. Verified still wrong on HEAD.
